_meta.description: Deck craft rules for the /deck skill. Machine-readable; every rule follows the uniform schema below. Encodes Dontae's writing, copy, and design decisions from the POSE deck production process (June 2026).

copy_rules.rule: Never write slide headlines in the two-sentence 'Statement. Punchy fragment.' formula.
copy_rules.why: Recognized AI writing pattern ('AI slopism') that mimics startup pitch decks. Reads as formulaic.
copy_rules.test: headline must NOT match /^[A-Z].+[.!?]\s+[A-Z][^.!?]{1,30}[.!?]?$/ — a declarative sentence followed by a short punchy fragment
bad: Social has been given the brief. Not the system.
The brief gets smarter every time the cycle closes. Most teams break it.
good: The operating model is what social is missing.
The brief no longer starts with the media plan.

copy_rules.rule: Do not communicate by negation. State what it IS, or use an explicit FROM→TO transformation. Do not lead with 'Not X', 'isn't X', or 'no longer X' as the primary message.
copy_rules.why: Negation centers and repeats the wrong idea. Reads as defensive. FROM→TO is the approved transition pattern.
copy_rules.test: copy must NOT open a statement with 'Not X', 'isn't X', or 'no longer X' as the slide's primary assertion — 'no longer' used mid-sentence within a FROM→TO construction that leads with the positive is permitted
good: POSE is one connected growth system
FROM: four disconnected channels → TO: one connected growth system
The brief no longer starts with the media plan. It starts with the cultural signal.
copy_rules.exception: Verbatim protected lines (e.g. 'Creators are not a media buy') stay as written. 'No longer' is acceptable mid-sentence in a FROM→TO construction that leads with the positive.

copy_rules.rule: Do not put abstract systems nouns front and center: 'infrastructure', 'the system / operating system', 'agentic', 'layer'.
copy_rules.why: Infrastructure is real but invisible. Systems-speak makes decks generic, not marketing-specific.
copy_rules.test: hero lines and sub lines must NOT lead with: infrastructure, agentic, the system, the layer, operating system (exception: 'operating model' where it is literally the product being sold)
bad: Every surface where culture becomes a cart
good: Creators earn the attention paid can only rent
Listening tells you what's working before the report does
Where people discover is now where they buy

copy_rules.rule: No em dashes (—) in any copy.
copy_rules.why: Hard formatting rule. Use commas, periods, colons, semicolons, parentheses, or line breaks instead.
copy_rules.test: copy must NOT contain the em dash character '—'
bad: Connected social makes paid work harder — every time the cycle closes.
good: Connected social makes paid work harder. Every time the cycle closes.

copy_rules.rule: Do not use banned words or phrases.
copy_rules.why: These terms are generic, vague, AI-sounding, or conflate distinct marketing disciplines.
copy_rules.test: copy must NOT contain any term from banned_words (exact token match, case-insensitive, ignoring scope notes)

banned_word_notes.authenticity: banned when used as vague rationale with no behavior named
banned_word_notes.holistic: allowed only when components are explicitly defined
banned_word_notes.ecosystem: allowed only when components are explicitly listed
banned_word_notes.signals: allowed only when the specific behavior or data point is named

preferred_alternatives.authenticity: name the actual behavior — creator participation, audience trust
preferred_alternatives.signals: name the specific behavior or data point

good: scale creator content as paid
earn audience trust through creator participation
use the platform's strength

copy_rules.rule: Headlines are conclusions, not labels. Start with the answer, then context.
copy_rules.why: Deck slides must earn their place. A label is not a headline. A conclusion is.
copy_rules.test: headline must assert a specific, arguable claim — not simply name a section or topic
good: POSE turns four channels into one compounding system
Paid works harder when owned, shared, and earned feed it proof
The measurement model is the operating model

copy_rules.rule: Name the marketing discipline precisely. Do not use soft verbs for channels.
copy_rules.why: Soft verbs (Listen, Watch, Amplify) flatten distinct disciplines. Precision signals strategic fluency.
copy_rules.test: channel references must use the discipline name, not a soft verb standing alone
good: social listening + signal detection (SENSE)
social monitoring + trend tracking
run as paid / scale creator content

mapping.Listen: social listening + cultural and market intelligence + signal detection (SENSE)
mapping.Amplify: scale, extend, or run as paid — never amplify

copy_rules.rule: The POSE deck POV is: 'Connected social makes paid work harder.' Never denigrate paid.
copy_rules.why: Spark is the #1 paid social agency in the Publicis network. The deck is pro-paid and forward.
copy_rules.test: no slide should frame paid as the weak, wasteful, or failing element — paid is always the accelerant
good: paid compounding on the proof the system feeds it
connected social makes paid work harder
copy_rules.approved_pov: FROM: paid carrying the load alone. TO: paid compounding on the proof the system feeds it.
copy_rules.approved_reframe: When a layer fails, the failure is DISCONNECTION, never paid being weak. Paid is the accelerant that compounds when the system feeds it proof and ready audiences.

copy_rules.rule: Short bullet items do not end with periods. Do not over-template with markdown.
copy_rules.why: Periods on short bullets add visual noise. Consistency with reference deck style.
copy_rules.test: bullet items under 8 words should NOT end with a period
good: Social listening
Creator participation
POSE framework

design_rules.rule: Approximately one dense anchor slide per 6 lighter slides.
design_rules.why: Dontae: 'for every 6 basic slides you need a dense slide. more content on slides!'
design_rules.test: deck must have at least 1 dense anchor for every 6 standard slides; flag if ratio exceeds 1:8
good: Slide 10: Why Publicis (dense)
Slide 17: The POSE Stack (dense)
Slide 27: The Diagnostic (dense)
Slide 32: Commerce (dense)

design_rules.rule: Dense anchor slides must use the full toolkit: layers, left rail, spine bar, hero glow, platform tags, right rail, legend.
design_rules.why: The toolkit is derived from the 4 reference architectures (Publicis PESO Stack, Agentic PESO OS, Measurement OS, Orbital PESO).
design_rules.test: a dense slide must have at least: left rail labels, a spine/master bar, platform/partner tags on cards, and a legend or right rail
good: Left rail: numbered layers
Spine: 'POSE Orchestration' bar spanning width
Cards: Meta·TikTok·YouTube tags
Right rail: C-suite perspectives + legend

toolkit.architecture: Layered, top-down, with DOWNWARD CONNECTOR ARROWS between layers
toolkit.left_rail: Numbered or named layers with icon
toolkit.spine: A central colored SPINE bar (core/orchestration layer)
toolkit.hero_glow: GOLD or MAGENTA GLOW on the hero layer (Spark = magenta-glow POSE band)
toolkit.cards: Each card carries real PLATFORM/PARTNER tags + a short note
toolkit.right_rail: Side panels: principles, C-suite perspectives, or operating rhythm + LEGEND

design_rules.rule: Never exceed PESO Measurement OS density. Cut cells ~50% vs the densest references. Keep BIG BOLD type.
design_rules.why: Dontae: 'the attached are often too dense — strike a balance.'
design_rules.test: no single slide should have more cell count than 50% of the PESO Measurement OS reference (9 layers × many cells); big type must remain legible when projected
bad: 9 layers each with 5+ cells and 8pt type
good: 4 layers, 3 cards per layer, 24pt minimum type on dark background
design_rules.reference_ceiling: reference-peso-measurement-os — 9 layers × many cells per layer, tiny type
design_rules.target_balance: Between Publicis-Stack (clean) and Agentic-OS (rich). Aim at 70% of Agentic-OS density.
design_rules.enforcement: Density means more real substance (platforms, notes, structure), NOT smaller text.

design_rules.rule: LEAN-DARK brand system: dark background, magenta/gold accents, white/cream type.
design_rules.why: The Spark deck uses the lean-dark brand system. All dense slides must match it.
design_rules.test: no slide should use a white or light background; magenta is the hero accent, gold is the secondary accent
good: Dark background, magenta-glow POSE band, gold spine bar, white type

accent_colors.connector_arrows: solid for control, dotted for data, gold-dotted for feedback

design_rules.rule: Do not lean on whitespace. Fill the frame with real substance.
design_rules.why: Dontae: 'yours is too simple'. Empty-looking slides are underbuilt, not minimal.
design_rules.test: every slide must have a conclusion headline PLUS at least one of: data point, platform list, framework visual, or case reference
bad: Headline only, large empty space below
One bullet point on a full slide
good: Headline + 3 platform-tagged cards + right rail principle callouts

design_rules.rule: Every slide must have a distinct POV. Cut duplicative thin slides.
design_rules.why: Dontae: 'every survivor needs a distinct POV, not a simple diagram saying nothing'
design_rules.test: no two slides should assert the same claim at the same level of abstraction
bad: Two consecutive slides both titled variations of 'The POSE Framework'
good: Slide A: POSE framework overview. Slide B: How each quadrant compounds paid.

design_rules.rule: Architecture slides use DOWNWARD CONNECTOR ARROWS between layers, not bidirectional or implied.
design_rules.why: Top-down flow reflects the activation hierarchy: capability → orchestration → measurement → activation.
design_rules.test: connector arrows on architecture slides must point downward; no horizontal-only or upward arrows unless explicitly showing a feedback loop
good: Arrows from capability cards down into orchestration spine, then down into activation
design_rules.reference: reference-publicis-peso-stack: capability cards → orchestration spine → measurement/governance → activation

design_rules.rule: Dense architecture slides must include real platform/partner tags on cards, not generic labels.
design_rules.why: Real names (Meta, Epsilon, LiveRamp) signal credibility and specificity. Generic labels are placeholders.
design_rules.test: every card on a dense slide must name at least one real platform or partner
good: Paid Social: Meta·TikTok·YouTube·Snap
Identity: Epsilon·LiveRamp

structure_rules.rule: All deck work happens in Figma. Always invoke /deck skill first, then read the deck index from the Figma file via MCP.
structure_rules.why: Figma is the single source of truth for slide state. Any edit outside Figma creates drift.
structure_rules.test: no slide mutation may occur without an active Figma MCP session and a confirmed deck index read
bad: Editing slide copy in a text editor and pasting into Figma
good: Read index via Figma MCP → confirm slide → edit in Figma → update index

structure_rules.rule: The slide index (CSV + index v5 in Figma) is the source of truth for slide count, order, and status. Never trust a verbal count.
structure_rules.why: Verbal counts drift. The index is the authoritative record — it gates every slide decision.
structure_rules.test: before any slide addition, removal, or reorder, the index must be read and confirmed current
bad: 'The deck has about 45 slides' without reading the index
good: Index read: 45 slides, last updated [date] — confirmed before edit

structure_rules.rule: Always invoke /deck skill first before making any slide changes. The skill holds state, POV, and index path.
structure_rules.why: The /deck skill loads the current Figma file key, POV, and index path. Without it, edits are stateless.
structure_rules.test: /deck must be invoked and confirm Figma file key before any slide read or write
bad: Going straight to Figma MCP without invoking /deck
good: /deck → confirms WTCQXah5tjh94gp4JfYagW + index path → Figma MCP edit

protected_verbatim_lines: Creators are not a media buy. They are translation infrastructure.
The product is not a deck. The product is repeatable judgment.
The earned question is not did we get coverage? It is what can that authority make more believable?

pose_pov.approved_pov: Connected social makes paid work harder.
pose_pov.paid_framing: Paid built modern social and moves the most. Frame paid as the accelerant that compounds when the system feeds it proof and ready audiences.
pose_pov.channel_name: SENSE (not Listen) is the correct step name in the POSE cycle
pose_pov.discipline_precision: Name the discipline: 'social listening + cultural and market intelligence + signal detection' — not just 'Listen'

publicis_peso_stack.pattern: logo/year/note cards → spine bar → activation, downward arrows
publicis_peso_stack.note: Good structural reference — too simple on its own

agentic_peso_os.pattern: dark+gold layered template, master bar, glow hero layer, right-rail legend
agentic_peso_os.note: Matches LEAN-DARK brand; aim for this density

peso_measurement_os.note: Adopt the structure, cut cells ~50%, use big type

peso_orbital_infographic.pattern: central-metaphor diagram + legend + edge callouts + formula band + playbook/outcomes
